findings: 2NTEx branch condition 'partner_hcp <= 1 and opps_combined_hcp >= 17' is a tautology in disguise: with hero holding 22, partner <= 1 forces opps >= 17 on every layout, so EVERY partner-bust layout plays 2NT doubled. Doubling a 20-21 2NT vulnerable needs a trailing opponent with real values/tricks; retightened to 'partner_hcp <= 1 and south_hcp >= 11' so busts are only sometimes punished (T2 doubled branch retained).

2C tree drives to 6NT at partner_hcp >= 10 (combined 32): after 2C-2D-2NT (22-24) partner's 10-11 is a quantitative invite that a bare 22 declines — patched to 4NTE at 10-11, 6NTE at 12+. As written, 2C was saddled with failing 32-point vulnerable slams, a baked-in tax on the option.
2NT slam floor (partner 11 -> 6NT) is correct as-is: hero's 22 is a maximum for 20-21 and accepts every invite; the asymmetric floors (11 vs 12) are the honest deviation consequence, not tuning.
Game floors 5 (over 2NT) vs 3 (over 2C) correctly encode the real dilemma (thin games missed after the underbid) — good.

Options fine: 2NT and 2C-then-2NT are the only mainstream calls with 22 balanced on a standard card (the c-room's 2D is presumably conventional and not offerable here); deviation flag on 2NT (one point above 20-21) is correctly 'judgment', not card_violation.

Residual limitation accepted rather than patched: neither tree models opponent jamming over 2C (more room than over 2NT), but with 18 HCP split among three hands the landing spots would barely change; noting, not branching.
Stem note, option notes free of verdict leakage; explanation draft factual (22 HCP, shapes check out) — lightly extended to mention the quantitative-decline cost now modelled.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees cleanly.

N.note: Dealt and passed: no opening bid, roughly 0-11 HCP.

2NT.note: 22 HCP is a point above the 20-21 card range; partner will value game and slam tries a point light.

stem_explanations: Pass — North has no opening bid (roughly 0-11 HCP).

2NT.partner: Partner passes with a bust, uses transfers/Stayman with a suit, raises to game with about 5+, and tries for slam with 11+.

2C.shows: Artificial and strong; the planned 2NT rebid shows 22-24 balanced.
2C.partner: Partner must reply once (2D waiting); after the 2NT rebid partner bids game with as little as 3 and looks for slam with about 10+.

patched_doc.explanation: East holds a balanced 22-count (A-K-5, K-J-2, A-Q-4-2, K-Q-7) after North passes. 2NT (20-21) is a one-point underbid that saves a round of bidding space; the cost is missing thin games when partner holds 3-4 HCP and playing partner's invitations a point light. 2C followed by 2NT shows the full 22-24 but consumes room and invites more aggressive continuations from partner, including quantitative slam tries East must judge with a bare minimum.

findings: P tree kills all E-W competition once North opens (north_hcp>=12 branches fire first): East with 14+ and a five-card suit sells out to 1NTN while the 1D tree lets East act from 6 HCP — classic asymmetric vigor favouring the author's opening line.
P tree gives hero no re-entry at all: an 11-count with both red suits never balances over a dying third-seat opening; 'hero always sells out' is a baked-in penalty against Pass.

1D tree prices no deviation downside despite the judgment flag's own note ('partner may drive too high'): game only at east_hcp>=14 (combined 25 — a normal game), so opening light is never punished; lowered the drive-to-game floor to 13 (combined 24, partner playing hero for 12+).
Both trees ignore the 4-4 heart fit hero can see half of: no 4HE/3HE/2HE fit landings anywhere despite hero holding A973; 1D tree parks every east 6-13 non-5-card-major hand in 1NTE.
1D tree lets a strong balanced North (14-18) vanish — no 1NT-overcall/takeout-double branch; only 5-card-major overcalls modelled.

1D tree floor 'east_hcp>=11 and east_spades>=5 -> 2SE' leaves a 13-count with five spades (2/1 game force opposite an opening bid) in a partscore; mitigated by the new east>=13 game branches ahead of it.
Meanings, options, deviation kind (judgment for an 11-count opening, correct — one point light, not a zone), stem notes and option notes are all fine; no verdict leakage in pre-answer text.
Patched projections pass validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state and lint_projection_trees with no warnings (all T3 floors within tolerance, e.g. 2HE 11 vs 9, 1NTN 12 vs 14).

S.note: Dealt and passed: no opening bid, roughly 0-11 HCP.

1D.note: 11 HCP is below a sound opening bid; partner will play West for 12+ and may drive too high.

stem_explanations: Pass — South has no opening bid (roughly 0-11 HCP).

P.shows: No opening bid in second seat — fewer than 12 HCP or unsuitable shape.
P.partner: Partner will place West with under opening values; East may still open in fourth seat and West must then limit the hand.

1D.shows: An opening bid, 12-21 HCP with 3+ (usually 4+) diamonds.
1D.partner: Partner responds normally and will invite or drive toward game with 11-12+, expecting a full opener.

patched_doc.explanation: Second seat, both vulnerable, with a square 11-count: 9-7-5 spades, A-9-7-3 hearts, K-5-4-3 diamonds, A-6 clubs. Two aces and a working king argue for opening 1D despite the raw count; the flat 3-4-4-2 shape and lack of spot texture argue for passing and letting the auction come back. Passing risks being shut out or having to balance later with limited safety; opening risks partner overvaluing the hand at both-vulnerable and driving to a thin game.

findings: OPTION SET: the binary 2H / XX is correct here - pass (playing 2Dx) with T82 is not a panel vote, and no third call exists. Retransfer XX is mainstream expert practice; no deviation flags needed.

MEANINGS GAP (main): the N note asserts 'with good diamonds opener redoubles', so the systemic pass carries a negative inference about N's diamond honors - but the bands never modeled it. Added suit_hcp D [0,5] with a soft [6,7] tail for North. Without this, layouts where N holds KQJx of diamonds (which the note says are impossible after the pass) pollute both lines.

PROJECTION GAP: only East ever competed to 3D. West, the lead-directing doubler, is the most natural 3D competitor over 2H with a 6th diamond and a maximum pass; added the symmetric 3DW branch (west_diamonds >= 5 and west_hcp >= 9) to both trees. Floors identical across options, so no T3 asymmetry.

The core comparison is honest: identical 3D-competition branches in both trees, and the else leaves differ only by declarer (2HS vs 2HN) - exactly the lead-position question the spot is about, with no baked verdict.
NOTES: 7/7 stem notes accurate (transfer, lead-director, systemic pass all correctly glossed); option_explanations state both lead positions evenhandedly, no verdict hint; draft explanation factual.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees (no errors/warnings) and stem-note count.

N.note: 1NT: 15-17 balanced. Pass of the lead-directing double: systemic - denies three-card heart support (with three+ hearts opener completes the transfer; with good diamonds opener redoubles, so the pass also denies strong diamond honors).

W.note: Passed hand; double of the 2D transfer is lead-directing, showing length and real honor strength in diamonds.

stem_explanations: Transfer to hearts - five or more hearts, any strength.
Lead-directing: length and honors in diamonds, wants a diamond lead.
Systemic pass - only two hearts (completing would promise three), and no diamond stack to redouble with.
Nothing to add over the double.

2H.shows: Sign-off: a weak hand with five-plus hearts, content to declare 2H from responder's side.
2H.partner: Partner passes; the opening lead comes through the strong 1NT hand. Either opponent may still compete in diamonds.

XX.shows: Re-transfer: demands that opener bid 2H so the strong balanced hand declares and the lead runs up to it.
XX.partner: Partner is forced to bid 2H, which hero will pass; opener declares. Either opponent may still compete in diamonds.

patched_doc.explanation: After a transfer to hearts is doubled for a diamond lead and opener's pass denies three hearts, responder holds Q42 KJT86 T82 T3 and must still play 2H. The only real question is which side declares: bidding 2H directly puts the weak hand at the wheel, while the systemic redouble re-transfers so the 15-17 hand is protected from the marked diamond lead through it. Both rooms chose differently (2H vs XX), and the opponents may still compete in diamonds.

findings: FACTUAL ERROR in explanation: 'three outside aces' - Q85 AKQJ3 A9 A82 has THREE aces total but only TWO outside the running heart suit. Fixed to 'two outside aces'. The guide is explicit that every checkable fact must be right.

PROJECTION ASYMMETRY: the speculative solid-suit double of 3NT existed only in the 3NT tree. The 3H tree reaches the identical 3NTE contract (when West lacks heart support) and the same South with the same spade stack doubles there too; omitting it made the deviation line doubled and the systemic line not, on the same layouts - a baked-in tax on 3NT. Added the symmetric 3NTEx branch to the 3H tree.

That double's gate was also too loose (south_spades_hcp >= 7 and south_hcp >= 9 lets KQJxxx plus a stray queen double a 20-21 notrump into a possible redouble); tightened to south_spades_hcp >= 8 and south_hcp >= 10 in both trees.

MEANINGS: W's major bands [1,5] at full weight ignore the negative inference that with a five-card major West transfers rather than Puppets; capped at 4 with a soft 5-card tail (0.2) and rewrote the note to say Puppet also covers three-card support hunting opener's five-card major - which is exactly what the 3H tree's west_hearts >= 3 raise relies on.
DEVIATION FLAG correct: 3NT is a genuine card_violation (denies any 4/5-card major) and the option_explanations honestly state what partner will believe; no verdict leakage anywhere pre-answer.
Category retagged from 'other' to 'game' - both lines choose which game to play. Stem note 1 rephrased ('as a passed hand' described West's own first pass circularly).
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees (no errors/warnings) and stem-note count.

W.note: Passed hand. 3C over 2NT: Puppet Stayman - game-going values opposite 20-21, holding a four-card major or three-card support hunting a five-card major in opener's hand; with his own five-card major West would have transferred instead.

3NT.note: The Puppet 3NT response denies any four- or five-card major; hero conceals a five-card heart suit headed by AKQJ, so a 5-3 or 5-4 heart fit will be lost.

stem_explanations: 20-21 balanced; may contain a five-card major.
Puppet Stayman - looking for a four- or five-card major, game values.

3H.shows: The systemic answer: exactly five hearts.
3H.partner: Partner raises to 4H with three-card (or better) support, signs off in 3NT without it.

3NT.shows: No four-card and no five-card major - a pure notrump hand.
3NT.partner: Partner passes; any heart fit stays hidden and the strong hand declares 3NT with its majors undisclosed.

patched_doc.explanation: A 20-count with AKQJ3 of hearts opened 2NT and now faces Puppet Stayman from a passed-hand partner. The book call is 3H, finding every 5-3 fit; the alternative is a deliberate 3NT, denying a major, on the theory that a 5332 with a running suit and two outside aces plays notrump well and keeps the lead coming up to the tenaces. The two world-class rooms split exactly this way (3H vs 3NT).

findings: Deviation ledger empty, but on the assumed card a 17-count 4-4-3-2 is a system-perfect 1NT opening; choosing 1D carries a real negative inference (partner will never play West for 15-17 balanced) that the user should see flagged. Added an 'judgment' (info) flag on 1D and the matching inference to its option note. Not a card_violation — 1D is a legal convenient-minor opening in range.

Projection symmetry is genuinely good: partner reaches 4M/3NT at 8+ via both routes with the declarer honestly flipped (W after Stayman/transfers, E after 1D-1M-raise), and each option's true cost is modelled (1NT buries the 4-4 fit with partner 5-7; 1D's 16-18 jump raise gets overboard at 3M on the same hands). No baked-in verdict.

South — a PASSED, NON-vulnerable hand up to 11 HCP — was permanently silent in both trees; only North ever competed. Added symmetric South balancing/competition branches (6-card suit; floor 8 over 1NT vs 6 over 1D, within T3 tolerance and honestly harder over the strong NT).
North entry thresholds (11 HCP/6-card suit over 1NT vs 10/5 over 1D) are asymmetric for the right systemic reason (entering over a strong NT vs over one-of-a-minor); floors within tolerance.

Meanings fine (South 0-11 with 12-tail); options complete — no third mainstream call exists with 17 balanced; stem note and option notes factual, no verdict leakage; explanation draft factual (17 HCP, 4-4-3-2, no-rebid problem is real).

Minor accepted quirk: in the 1NT tree North's balance branches sit above partner's transfer branches, so a weak partner with a 5-card major occasionally loses the fit to 2SN — a defensible competitive outcome, left alone.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees with no warnings.

S.note: Dealt and passed: no opening bid, roughly 0-11 HCP.

1D.note: With 15-17 balanced the card opens 1NT; after 1D partner will play West for an unbalanced hand or a strength outside 15-17.

stem_explanations: Pass — South has no opening bid (roughly 0-11 HCP).

1NT.shows: 15-17 balanced; may contain one or two 4-card majors.
1NT.partner: Partner passes weak balanced hands, transfers with a 5-card major, and raises toward 3NT/game with about 8+.

1D.shows: An opening bid, 12-21 HCP, 3+ diamonds (exactly three only with 4-4-3-2); by inference tends to deny a 15-17 balanced hand, which would have opened 1NT.
1D.partner: Partner responds a 4-card major up the line; opener raises with 4-card support and shows 17 by inviting or driving on.

patched_doc.explanation: West holds A-K-9-8, A-8-6-5, K-6-4, K-2 — 17 HCP, 4-4 in the majors, 4-4-3-2 — after South passes. Opening 1NT describes the strength in one bid and protects the tenaces, but can bury a 4-4 major fit unless partner can use Stayman. Opening 1D (convenient three-card suit) keeps both majors in play via cheap raises, but the hand has no comfortable rebid over a 2-level response and may get overboard or underbid later.

findings: OPTION SET correct: 4D / 4S-cue / 5D is the panel menu; pass is not on the card (4C forcing), 5C on 64 doubleton and 4NT with two small clubs are no one's votes. No deviation flags needed and none given.

MEANINGS GAP (main): the trees keep N-S silent forever, which is only coherent because North declined to raise spades TWICE (over 3D and over the forcing 4C) at favorable-ish colors - a sharpened failure-to-raise inference the guide says the author must add, and the doc never modeled. Added an N seat entry: spade support usually 0-2 (soft 3-4 at 0.3). Without it, simulated Norths with 4-card support who would have jumped to 4S populate the layouts and the quiet trees become a fiction.

PROJECTION: E's 6C drive was gated at east_clubs >= 6 and east_hcp >= 14 only - KQxxxx and out drives a possible 6-2 club slam. Added east_clubs_hcp >= 8 so 'insisting on clubs' requires a self-sufficient suit.

PROJECTION: over the minimum 4D, an East with 14+, six clubs and at most two diamonds simply passed (the 5C continuation required seven clubs). That East has clear extras and no diamond tolerance - added a 5CE branch for him; (E,C,5) floors remain 12 across options.

The vigor asymmetry between trees (E raises to 5D over 4D at 14+ but drives 6D over the cue at the same 14+) is informationally justified - hero's calls carry different messages - and the T3 floors are consistent; this is the rare tree set that models information transfer honestly.

NOTES: 5/5 stem notes accurate; option_explanations honest (the 4S note correctly explains why the cue cannot be natural); explanation factual - AJT9 does sit over the preempter on hero's right, and no verdict leaks.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees (no errors/warnings) and stem-note count.

S.note: Weak two: six spades (occasionally a good five), 5-10 HCP, nonvulnerable.

E.note: 4C over the 3D overcall: natural, good five-plus (usually six) clubs, forcing one round, constructive values - roughly opening strength, typically diamond tolerance.

N.note: Passed over both 3D and the forcing 4C: with three-plus spades and any shape North would have raised the preempt at these colors, so he is usually short in spades.

stem_explanations: Overcall: good five-plus diamonds, opening values or better.
Natural clubs, forcing - a good suit and constructive values opposite the overcall.

4D.shows: Rebiddable six-card diamonds, a minimum overcall - the mildest continuation, passable by agreement.
4D.partner: Partner passes with an ordinary 4C bid, raises to 5D or insists on clubs with extras.

4S.shows: A cue-bid of the enemy suit: first-round spade control and slam interest with a minor-suit fit - it cannot be natural facing a weak two on hero's right.
4S.partner: Partner is forced onward: cue-bids or drives to six with extras, otherwise signs off at the five level in a minor.

5D.shows: A self-sufficient diamond suit and a hand upgraded by the club fit - game bid, mild slam suggestion.
5D.partner: Partner passes normally, raising to 6D only with clear extras and diamond support.

patched_doc.explanation: Over South's nonvulnerable weak 2S, hero overcalled 3D on AJT9 9 AQJ762 64 and partner forced with 4C. The spade AJT9 sits over the preempter and the hand has six strong diamonds with a singleton heart: the choice is a minimum-sounding 4D, a spade cue-bid agreeing a minor and inviting slam, or a direct jump to 5D. Both world-class rooms chose differently (4D vs 4S).

findings: X tree had North's runout incentives INVERTED: north ran from 1NTx with 6+ HCP and sat with a bust. Standard theory is the opposite — a weak advancer scrambles to any 5-card suit, a value-holding advancer sits (or redoubles) since N-S then own the balance. As written, N-S escaped exactly when sitting was good for them and sat exactly when running was right, which shields the doubling side from 1NTx-making disasters while also handing it cheap undertricks — a structurally corrupt branch pair, not a nitpick. Patched to north_hcp <= 7 runouts; strong Norths now sit into the else 1NTSx, where DD prices the made-doubled nightmare of doubling on 6 HCP.
2H tree raised partner straight to a VULNERABLE 4H on 16 HCP opposite an advertised weak, non-forcing 2H (combined ~22). Split into 16-17 -> 3HW (invite, hero's 6-count declines) and 18+ -> 4HW.

Everything else holds up: meanings for the 1C opener (11-21, 3+C, 5cM denials with leak) and the 1NT overcall (15-18 with tails, club-stopper suit_hcp band) are textbook; option set P/2H/X is complete for this position (new suits natural non-forcing, X penalty over their 1NT); the judgment flag on X (6 HCP with a running suit vs the 9+ card meaning, trick-based double) is the right kind and the 2HWx branch prices 2H's vulnerable risk symmetrically.
N-S vigor symmetric across P and 2H trees (3NTS floor 8, 2SN floor 5 in both); partner's reopening in the P tree (17+ with 6 clubs) is conservative but defensible.
Fixed 'save-haven' typo in the explanation draft; otherwise factual (6 HCP, AJT952, 3-points-light double, vulnerability named correctly) and verdict-free.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees with no warnings (the <=7 runout gates carry hcp terms for T1 and set no conflicting >= floors for T3).

E.note: 1C: natural, 3+ clubs, 11-21 HCP; denies a 5-card major.

S.note: 1NT overcall: 15-18 balanced with at least one club stopper.

X.note: The penalty double normally shows about 9+ HCP; this is 6 with a long suit — it banks on the hearts running and partner's opening values.

stem_explanations: 1NT — direct overcall, 15-18 balanced with a club stopper.

P.shows: No suit worth showing and no interest in penalizing 1NT.
P.partner: Partner will pass or reopen only with a shapely maximum; 1NT is likely to end the auction.

2H.shows: Natural and competitive: a 5-6 card heart suit in a weak hand, not forcing.
2H.partner: Partner passes with most hands and raises only with a fitting maximum; the auction usually dies in 2H.

X.shows: Penalty, about 9+ HCP sitting over the 1NT bidder, suggesting our side holds the balance of strength.
X.partner: Partner is expected to pass and defend 1NT doubled with any balanced hand; opponents may run to a suit.

patched_doc.explanation: Partner opens 1C and RHO overcalls a strong 1NT (15-18); West holds T-7, A-J-T-9-5-2, J, 6-4-3-2 — 6 HCP but a six-card heart suit headed by A-J-T-9. 2H is natural and competitive, taking the safe-haven partscore while the suit is biddable at the two level. Pass sells out to 1NT, hoping to beat it on heart leads. The penalty double maximizes the score when 1NT is going down but is made two to three points light and East-West are vulnerable if the runout or redouble goes wrong.

findings: OPTION SET correct: 3S (extra spades), 3H (stopper probe), 4D (fit + slam interest) is exactly the expert menu; 3NT with a stiff heart rightly excluded. The card_violation flag on 3H is right - in a GF minor-fit auction 3H shows heart values/stopper for 3NT and hero holds a singleton.

PROJECTION ASYMMETRY (main): 6D existed only in the 4D tree (north_hcp >= 15). The same 15+ North who drives slam over 4D was made to sign off in game after 3S or 3H - a one-sided slam zone that quietly bakes in 4D's upside/downside as unique. Added the identical 6D branch (north_hcp >= 15 and north_diamonds >= 4) to the 3S and 3H trees; floors now symmetric at 15.

DEVIATION DOWNSIDE: the 3NTx branch is well-conceived (West with hearts stacked doubles 3NT after hero's phantom 3H probe, directing the killing lead), but its gate west_hearts >= 6 and west_hearts_hcp >= 6 describes a hand (KQJxxx+) that usually overcalls 2H over 1S, leaving the branch nearly dead - present for the linter, not the model. Loosened to west_hearts_hcp >= 5 so sound-style passes (KJTxxx, KQxxxx) populate it.
MEANINGS sound: 2C GF 12+ (soft 11), 3D 4+ diamonds with the negative inference about three-card spade support stated and priced (S band 0-2 with soft 3 at 0.3).
NOTES: 8/8 stem notes accurate; option_explanations honest (3H's 'shows' states what partner will believe, which is the priced risk); draft explanation factual, no verdict leakage.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees (no errors/warnings) and stem-note count.

N.note: 2C: game-forcing, 4+ clubs, 12+ HCP (good 11 possible). 3D: 4+ diamond support, minor-oriented, typically at most two spades (three-card support usually shows spade preference instead).

3H.when: west_hearts >= 6 and west_hearts_hcp >= 5 and north_hearts_hcp >= 4 and north_hcp >= 12

3H.note: In a game-forcing minor-fit auction a new suit at the three level shows a stopper or real values there for 3NT; hero holds a singleton heart, so partner will bid 3NT with as little as Qx of hearts.

stem_explanations: Second suit - four or more diamonds, unlimited.
Diamond raise, setting the minor-suit fit; game force continues.

3S.shows: Extra spade length/quality - a five-card suit good enough to suggest spades as trumps despite partner's minor orientation.
3S.partner: Partner raises to 4S with a doubleton honor, bids 3NT with hearts under control, drives the diamond slam with real extras, or falls back on the diamond fit.

3H.shows: Heart values or a stopper, inviting partner to bid 3NT with complementary help there.
3H.partner: Partner bids 3NT with any heart help, drives the diamond slam with real extras, otherwise continues describing toward 5D or a spade contract.

4D.shows: A clear diamond raise with slam-suitable values - prime cards and no interest in 3NT.
4D.partner: Partner cue-bids toward 6D with extras, signs off in 5D with a balanced game force. Note 3NT is no longer available.

patched_doc.explanation: After 1S-2C; 2D-3D (game forcing), opener holds KT962 6 AK75 Q87. The strain question is acute: 3S offers the five-card major, 3H probes for 3NT despite the singleton heart, and 4D commits to the 4-4 diamond fit with slam interest. Both world-class rooms chose differently (3H vs 3S).

findings: PROJECTION BUG (worst): in the 2D (new-minor) tree, opener with three spades and a MINIMUM landed in 3SN. After NMF opener shows three spades at the two level (2S) and an invitational hero passes - the tree forced the NMF line a level too high on every minimum-fit layout, an artificial tax on the textbook invite route. Fixed to 2SN.

PROJECTION: the P tree sold out to 2HE whenever East balanced, but hero holds AJ943 - passing 1NT and then selling to a balancing 2H is not how this hand plays; hero competes 2S. Reworked: East balance -> hero 2S -> East on to 3H only with a sixth heart and the nine-card fit, mirroring the direct-2S tree so P and 2S face identical enemy vigor.

PROJECTION ASYMMETRY: East acted only in the P and 2S trees; over 2D/2NT the opponents always sold out, so the invitational lines lived in an artificially quiet room. Added the same 3HE competition branch (east_hcp >= 10, 6 hearts, 9-card fit) to the 2D and 2NT trees on minimum layouts; (E,H,3) floor now 10 in all four trees.

MEANINGS: opener's club band [2,6] contradicted the note twice - 1C promises 3+ clubs (min 3, not 2) and a 12-14 hand with six clubs rebids 2C, not 1NT (max 5). Fixed to [3,5]. Rest of the bands (S 1-3, H 1-4, D 2-4, 12-14 soft 11) are right.

DEVIATIONS: considered flagging 2D/2NT as judgment (hero is 9 facing a 10-11 invite) but left clean - a one-point upgrade on a chunky five-card suit is normal expert evaluation, the option notes already disclose '10-11' so the user sees the stretch, and a flag would force fake doubled branches into auctions no one doubles.
NOTES: 6/6 stem notes accurate; option_explanations honest (2D partner line adjusted to match the corrected tree: stop in 2S/2NT, not 3S); draft explanation factual, no verdict leakage.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees (no errors/warnings) and stem-note count.

S.note: 1C: 3+ clubs, 11-21. 1NT rebid: 12-14 balanced, fewer than four spades (would raise 1S), no four-card heart reverse shape, at most five clubs (with six he rebids 2C).

P.shows: A hand with no game interest opposite 12-14 and no urge to look for a better partscore.
P.partner: The auction ends unless fourth seat balances; over a 2H balance hero's five-card suit competes to 2S.

2S.shows: To play: five or more spades in a hand not worth an invitation.
2S.partner: Partner passes essentially always; only the opponents can revive the auction.

2D.shows: New-minor forcing: invitational or better values, usually five spades, asking for three-card support and range.
2D.partner: Partner shows three spades (minimum or maximum) or denies support and shows range; hero can stop in 2S/2NT or reach game.

2NT.shows: A natural balanced invitation, about 10-11 points, only four spades promised.
2NT.partner: Partner passes with 12 and poor 13-counts, bids 3NT with a maximum.

patched_doc.explanation: Responder holds AJ943 Q85 93 Q64 after 1C-1S; 1NT (12-14). Nine soft HCP with a chunky-ish five-card spade suit: the menu runs from quitting in 1NT, through the safety play of 2S, to inviting - either with natural 2NT or via new-minor 2D to check on three-card spade support first. The two rooms split between pass and 2S, and the invitational routes are the textbook third and fourth calls.

findings: South (an unpassed hand, VULNERABLE) jumped to 4HS on 12 HCP with six hearts in the 2S and 3S trees, yet stayed silent forever in the 2NT tree. Both halves are wrong: no vulnerable 12-count enters at the four level over a limit raise, and jamming over the two cheap raises but never over Jacoby is asymmetric vigor that taxes the raises relative to the author-flagged 2NT. Patched: over 2S South's 3H pushes E-W to 3SW (hero holds four trumps and competes); over 3S the 4-level entry is deleted.

2NT tree's mandatory doubled branch hinged on 'north_spades >= 4' — with opener promising 5+ and hero holding 4, North needs ALL four remaining spades (South void). A near-dead branch that exists only to satisfy T2, and a 4-trump-small double is not how 4S gets doubled anyway. Replaced with the honest disaster case: opener dead-minimum (partner_hcp <= 11) and opponents holding 16+ combined double the fake game force's 4S.

2S tree accepted game tries from partner_hcp >= 15 while its own option note says partner tries with 16-17 — tree and disclosure contradicted each other, and 15 flatters the underbid. Floor raised to 16 (hero's 11 is a maximum single raise and accepts every try).

3S tree acceptance was HCP-only (14+); added opener's shape acceptance (12+ with a 6th spade -> 4SW), which the option note ('14+ or extra shape') already promised. Branch ordered so the recorded T3 floor stays 14 (cross-option 4SW floors 16/14/16, within tolerance).

Kept and endorsed: 6SW at partner 18+ in the Jacoby tree (the a-king-light slam drive is precisely the flagged deviation's cost); the judgment kind for an 11-count Jacoby upgrade (2 points shy, style not zone breach); meanings for the 1S opener with 10-tail; both stem notes (North's pass correctly stated as denying overcall/double).
Residual, accepted: North never balances over a dying 2S — a shape-perfect competitive double exists there, but South's 3H push now covers most N-S competition mass.
Option notes and explanation draft factual (11 HCP, 4-3-3-3, both red aces) with no verdict leakage; patched doc passes all mechanical checks with no warnings.

2NT.note: Jacoby 2NT is a game force, normally 13+; this is an 11-count upgraded on two aces — partner will drive to game and evaluate slam a king light.

stem_explanations: Pass — North denies a sound overcall or takeout double of 1S.

2S.shows: A single raise: 6-10 support points with 3+ spades.
2S.partner: Partner passes most minimums, makes a game try with about 16-17, and drives to game with 18+.

3S.shows: A limit raise: 10-12 support points with 4 trumps, invitational.
3S.partner: Partner passes a minimum and bids 4S with about 14+ or extra shape.

2NT.partner: Partner shows shortness or extras and never stops below 4S; with a strong hand partner will look for slam.

patched_doc.explanation: Partner opens 1S, North passes, and East holds J-8-3-2, A-9-7, A-8-4, Q-7-2: 11 HCP, four trumps, both red aces, 4-3-3-3 shape. 3S is the textbook limit raise, inviting game. 2S treats the flat shape and soft queen-jack points as a downgrade, keeping the auction low. Jacoby 2NT upgrades the two aces and fourth trump into an outright game force, gaining when opener is minimum-plus but shapely, and risking a hopeless game or an overcooked slam auction when opener is dead minimum.

findings: OPTION SET fine: 3S/3NT/3H are exactly the three strains a panel would weigh; correctly no deviation flags (3S shows the actual three-card support, 3NT has the diamond AQx, 3H is a real extra-quality suit).

PROJECTION FLAW (main): North's band allows three hearts, hero has advertised six, yet no tree ever lets North convert to the known 6-3 heart fit. Over 3S every 12+ North with fewer than five spades raises to a Moysian 4S (even holding three hearts); over 3NT North with three hearts and game values always passes. This quietly biases the comparison toward 4S/3NT and away from the heart strain reaching game.
PROJECTION nit: 3S tree's third branch bids a 4-3 4S on any 12-count; acceptable only once the 3-card-heart hands are peeled off first.

MEANINGS: bands sensible ([11,18] with soft 10 for a forcing 3C), but the note missed the standard negative inference that a balanced 11-12 rebids 2NT instead of 3C, so 3C implies genuine shape - added to the note text.

NOTES/EXPLANATION: stem notes accurate call-by-call (9/9); option_explanations honest and verdict-free; draft explanation factual. The 3S option_explanation's partner line promised a 4H conversion path ('or stops in 3S') that the original tree did not actually contain - the patch makes the tree match the prose.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees (no errors/warnings) and stem-note count.

N.note: 1S: 4+ spades, 6+ HCP, one round force. 3C: natural 4+ clubs, forcing, game interest opposite a minimum 2H rebid; usually fewer than three hearts and no primary diamond suit. With a balanced 11-12 North would have rebid 2NT, so 3C implies real shape.

stem_explanations: Four or more spades, 6+ HCP, one round force.
Natural clubs, forcing - game interest, looking for the right strain.

3S.shows: Exactly three-card spade support (1S may have been four), minimum shape-suitable hand.
3S.partner: Partner picks the game: raises to 4S with five spades or extra values, converts to 4H with three-card heart support, offers 3NT with hearts short and the other suits held, or stops in 3S with a dead minimum.

3NT.shows: A minimum with the unbid suit (diamonds) under control and no three-card spade support message; offers to play right there.
3NT.partner: Partner normally passes; pulls to 4S with extreme spade-oriented shape or to 4H with concealed three-card heart support.

3H.shows: Extra heart length and quality - a suit playable opposite a small doubleton or less.
3H.partner: Partner raises to 4H with a doubleton honor or better, tries 3NT with a singleton heart and extras, or passes with a misfitting minimum.

patched_doc.explanation: Opener holds 954 AKT976 AQ7 5 after 1H-1S; 2H-3C (forcing). Three strains are live: three-card spade support for partner's possible five, 3NT on the diamond AQ plus partner's clubs, or 4H on the self-sufficient six-card suit. The singleton club and three small spades pull in different directions; both world-class rooms chose differently (3S vs 3NT).

findings: P tree let East's third-seat opening end N-S participation forever: with hero holding 12 and partner up to 20-odd, N-S never balanced or competed — 'hero always sells out' is a structural tax on Pass, the very option the tree is supposed to test. Added an N-S competitive branch (east opens, partner 11+ -> 2DN) so strong N-S layouts no longer donate 1NTE/2SE to the opponents.

1NT tree's doubled branch fired on east_hcp >= 14: nobody penalty-doubles a nominal 15-17 notrump on a flat 14-count (East cannot see hero's 12). Raised to 15 — the lie still gets caught, on honest frequency.

1NT tree sent partner_hcp >= 17 straight to 6NT: opposite a believed 15-17, a 16-17 count makes a quantitative invite that a 12-count declines. Patched to 18+ -> 6NTN, 16-17 -> 4NTN declined; the overreach cost of the psyche-range 1NT is now priced at the right level instead of overpriced.
1D tree parked partner's weak hands with a 5-card major in 1NTN; added 2SS/2HS landings (1D-1M-1NT-2M).

Correct and worth keeping: the card_violation flag on 1NT (12 vs 15-17 is a full zone — exactly the weak-NT-style mislabeling the guide warns about, labeled honestly); the game-floor asymmetry partner 13 (over 1D, 12-14 rebid) vs 9 (over 1NT, believed 15-17) is the honest price of the lie, not tuning; P-tree NT-by-South leaves are coherent (4th-seat 1m, opener's 1NT rebid).
East floors (12 to open in 3rd seat in the P tree, 10 to overcall in the 1D tree) are systemically sensible and within T3 tolerance.
Stem note, option notes, and draft explanation are factual (12 HCP, 3-3-4-3, ranges match) with no verdict leakage.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees with no warnings.

W.note: Dealt and passed: no opening bid, roughly 0-11 HCP.

1NT.note: 12 HCP is a full zone below the 15-17 card range; partner will invite and raise games three points light.

stem_explanations: Pass — West has no opening bid (roughly 0-11 HCP).

P.shows: No opening bid — fewer than 12 HCP or a hand judged too weak to open.
P.partner: Partner will not expect opening values; East gets a free run in third seat and South may open in fourth.

1D.partner: Partner responds a major up the line or bids notrump; opener's 1NT rebid limits the hand to 12-14.

1NT.partner: Partner passes weak hands, transfers with a 5-card major, invites with about 8 and raises to 3NT with 9+ — all counting North for 15-17.

patched_doc.explanation: Second seat after West's pass, North holds T-8-4, A-T-2, K-J-T-5, A-7-2: a square 12-count with two aces and strong intermediates. Opening 1D is the textbook action with a planned 12-14 1NT rebid. Passing treats the 3-3-4-3 shape and soft partscore potential as a sub-minimum. Opening 1NT is a weak-notrump-style bid that is three points below the partnership's 15-17 range: it preempts the opponents and right-sides notrump, but partner's invitations and raises will all be made a zone light.

findings: P tree sat 1NT doubled with a 0-count hero — at the table everyone scrambles; routed strong-N layouts to the 2H runout (doubled when N is loaded)
S's pass over 1NT carried a 13-15 tail at 0.4 — trap passing 15 over a strong NT is rare; narrowed to 13-14 at 0.2
E's 1NT allowed 5-card majors at full weight; demoted to a 0.3 style tail
N's penalty double of 2D was available at 13 HCP with 4 trumps; raised to 14 with 5
balancing gates kept identical across both trees (T3 world consistency)

S.note: Passed over 1NT: no conventional or natural action; can still hold a fair balanced hand with no long suit.

2C.note: Garbage Stayman on a 0-count: West intends to pass any response, including 2D on three small — partner may expect at least invitational interest.

stem_explanations: Pass — South takes no action over 1NT; with no shapely hand South can still hold up to a fair balanced count.

P.shows: A weak hand with no 5-card major and no game interest; 1NT is where the auction rests unless the opponents act.
P.partner: Partner has finished describing the hand; if fourth seat doubles or balances, you scramble to your cheapest 4-card suit.

2C.shows: Stayman, asking for a 4-card major — usually with values or a weak hand prepared for any reply.
2C.partner: Partner answers 2D, 2H, or 2S; West passes whatever comes back, so the side plays a two-level partscore.

patched_doc.explanation: Partner opens a 15-17 1NT and South passes; West holds T-9-8, 9-6-5-3, J-T-2, 9-6-4 — a 0-count with 3-4-3-3 shape. The high cards are heavily with the other side (North and South hold 23-25 between them), so passing leaves partner to play 1NT, possibly doubled in the passout seat. Garbage Stayman escapes to a two-level suit partscore — any response can be passed, with 2D landing on three small trumps — trading declarer's extra notrump tricks for trump safety and auction interference.

findings: OPTION SET correct and complete: P / 3H / 4H is the whole panel menu after a garbage-Stayman fit is found; no deviation flags needed (shape-based invite/blast on support points is mainstream, not a card breach).

PROJECTION FLAW (main): the P tree's only competitive branch sold out to 2SW. Hero with four trumps, a stiff club and a known 4-4 fit competes to 3H over any 2S balance as a matter of course - ending the auction in 2SW bakes an artificial downside into P. Branch retargeted to 3HN (balance happens, hero competes, opponents are done at 17-19 combined HCP vul).

Same branch was also nearly dead as gated: West needs 5+ spades while hero holds five and North 2-4, leaving E-W at most six spades combined - kept as the rare live case but widened to either opponent is not possible cleanly, so the West gate stands; the realism fix is the final contract, not the gate.

MEANINGS: minor bands [2,5] at full weight overweight semi-balanced 4M5m hands in a 15-17 1NT; tightened to [2,4] with a soft 5-card tail at 0.3. Heart band [4,5] and spade band [2,4] are exactly right (4-4 majors answer hearts first - the note says so).

NOTES: 8/8 stem notes accurate including the passed-hand and light-Stayman inferences; option_explanations honest and verdict-free; draft explanation factual (5 spades, stiff club, AQ4, 6 HCP all check).
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees (no errors/warnings) and stem-note count.

N.note: 1NT: 15-17 balanced (third seat). 2H: Stayman response, four hearts (occasionally five); with 4-4 majors hearts is bid first, so four spades is still possible.

stem_explanations: Nothing to open - fewer than 11-12 HCP.
Stayman - at least one four-card major (may be light, planning to pass any response).
Four hearts, possibly also four spades.

P.shows: A weak hand that used Stayman only to escape 1NT - fit found, no game interest.
P.partner: Partner passes; the auction ends unless fourth seat balances, and over a balance hero's shape competes to 3H.

3H.shows: An invitational raise with four-card heart support, roughly 7-8 points once shape is counted.
3H.partner: Partner passes with 15 and most 16-counts, bids 4H with a maximum.

4H.shows: A raise to game - values or distribution that make 4H playable opposite any 15-17 with four hearts.
4H.partner: Partner always passes; hero has taken the final decision.

patched_doc.explanation: A passed hand with T8542 T986 AQ4 6 finds a 4-4 heart fit after Stayman over a 15-17 1NT. Only 6 HCP, but a fifth spade, a singleton club and a working AQ make the hand far bigger in support terms. The choice is how high to drive the fit: stop safely, invite, or blast game on shape. The two world-class rooms split between 3H and 4H.

findings: 2NT tree branch 'west_hcp >= 12 and west_diamonds >= 4 -> 5DW' bids a 21-combined-HCP game (hero 9 + west 12) on a possible 4-card fit: opposite a weak-or-strong unusual 2NT no sane West drives to the five level on 12 with four trumps. This one branch quietly taxes the 2NT option with routine -2s. Repriced to west 14+ with FIVE diamonds (11-card fit, support-point drive).

Asymmetric opponent vigor: P and 2D trees let N-S reach 3NTS at combined 25, the 2NT tree never did — strong misfitting N-S hands could only double the minor pick. Added the matching 3NTS branch (opps 25+, south short in spades).

2D tree never let partner act on strength: West with 13+ opposite an advertised 10-16 sound overcall drives to 3NT — which is simultaneously 2D's legitimate upside and, with hero's actual 9-count, the flagged deviation's second downside (the first, the 2DEx penalty branch, was already present and satisfies T2). Added west 13+ -> 3NTW.
Widened the minor-preference gate for the 2NT pick from west_hcp <= 11 to <= 13 so a 12-13-count West no longer lands in clubs while holding longer diamonds.

Sound as authored and kept: option set (P/2D/2NT is the complete panel menu; X is shape-illegal, 3C a non-starter); the judgment flag on 2D (fair 9-count, hides the second suit) with no flag on 2NT — 5-5 at none-vul is exactly what the card's UNT shows, honestly labeled; meanings for the 1S opener (11-21, 10-tail, 5-8 spades); hero's own balancing route in the P tree (west 10+ -> 2DE).
Stem note, option notes and explanation draft are factual (9 HCP, 5-5, singleton spade) and verdict-free.
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees with no warnings (2DEx/4SN/3NTS floors symmetric across trees).

2D.when: south_hcp >= 6 and south_spades >= 3 and west_hcp >= 8 and west_diamonds >= 3

2NT.when: south_hcp >= 10 and south_spades <= 2 and west_diamonds >= west_clubs

2D.note: A two-level overcall on a fair 9-count; many partnerships want sounder values, and it hides the second suit.

P.shows: Nothing worth showing over 1S at this vulnerability.
P.partner: Partner will not expect two playable suits; East may still balance later if the auction dies low.

2D.shows: A sound natural two-level overcall, 5+ diamonds, roughly 10-16.
2D.partner: Partner raises diamonds or competes with a fit; the club suit stays hidden unless East can bid again.

2NT.shows: Unusual notrump: at least 5-5 in the minors, typically weak or very strong, not a natural notrump bid.
2NT.partner: Partner must pick a minor at the three level with weak hands and can invite/jump with a fit; the partnership is committed to the three level.

patched_doc.explanation: Nobody vulnerable, East holds 3, J-5, K-Q-T-8-4, Q-J-9-4-3 over a 1S opening: 9 HCP, 5-5 in the minors, a singleton spade. The unusual 2NT shows both suits at once and pre-empts, but commits the side to the three level on a hand with modest high cards. A simple 2D overcall shows the better suit at a safer level but loses the clubs. Pass avoids the vulnerability-free but still real penalty risk and waits, at the cost of silence on a shapely hand.

findings: OPTION SET correct: 3H/4H is the entire expert menu (2S preference, 2NT and 3S are no one's vote with four trumps and a max semi-forcing 1NT); rightly no deviation flags - an 11-count with AK-K prime cards is a normal max for 1NT then invite/drive.

PROSE/TREE MISMATCH (main): the 3H option note says partner bids 4H 'with about 14+ or extra shape', but the tree accepted only on north_hcp >= 14. A 12-count 5-5 opener - the classic shape acceptance - always passed 3H, quietly under-accepting the invite relative to its own disclosed meaning. Added the shape-acceptance branch (north_hearts >= 5 and north_hcp >= 12).
MEANINGS sound: 2H band 11-17 (19+ hands jump), S 5-7 / H 4-6, and the semi-forcing 1NT inference (balanced minimums pass, so 2H implies real 5-4 shape) is correctly reflected by H >= 4.
PROJECTIONS otherwise sensible: opponent silence is realistic here (E-W hold at most ~17 combined, vulnerable, against a live invitational auction); no slam branch needed at 27-29 max combined.
NOTES: 6/6 stem notes accurate including the semi-forcing gloss; option_explanations honest and verdict-free; explanation facts all check (hand, 11 HCP, wide-ranging 11-17 2H, rooms split 3H/4H).
Patched doc passes validate_finalization, validate_options_against_state, lint_projection_trees (no errors/warnings) and stem-note count.

N.note: 1S: five or more spades, 11-21. 2H: second suit, four or more hearts, non-jump so roughly 11-17 - unlimited shapes with 5-4 or longer.

stem_explanations: Semi-forcing 1NT: 6-12 HCP, fewer than three spades or a hand unsuited to a raise.
Second suit: four or more hearts, minimum-to-medium range.

3H.shows: An invitational heart raise: four-card support with a maximum 1NT response, around 10-12 points.
3H.partner: Partner passes with a minimum opening, bids 4H with about 14+ or extra shape (a fifth heart).

4H.shows: A raise to game: four trumps and a hand hero judges worth ten tricks opposite any 5-4 opener.
4H.partner: Partner always passes; the decision is taken out of opener's hands.

patched_doc.explanation: After 1S-1NT; 2H, responder holds J2 K632 AK62 973: a maximum 1NT response with four trumps, two working diamond honors and a doubleton spade for ruffs. Opener's 2H is wide-ranging (11-17), so the question is whether 11 prime-ish points opposite an unlimited 5-4 justify driving to game or only inviting. The two world-class rooms split between 3H and 4H.
